trips.title: Bwindi Gorilla Trekking Experience
trips.summary: Includes a gorilla trekking permit, accommodation, full-board meals, return transport, an English-speaking guide, park fees, and bottled water. Price shown is the from rate for larger groups.

trips.title: Gorilla Trekking & Lake Bunyonyi Escape
trips.summary: Combine a lifetime gorilla encounter with a relaxing Lake Bunyonyi escape, scenic drives, local culture, and beautiful photography opportunities.

trips.title: Kidepo Valley National Park Adventure
trips.summary: Luxury accommodation, multiple game drives, a cultural visit, park fees, full-board meals, a professional guide, and Kampala–Kidepo return transport.

trips.title: Murchison Falls National Park Safari
trips.summary: Return transport, one night of accommodation, meals, a game drive, Nile boat cruise, top-of-the-falls visit, guide, bottled water, and photography stops.

trips.title: Lake Bunyonyi Wellness Retreat
trips.summary: Lake-view accommodation, canoe ride, island visit, sunrise and sunset experiences, guided wellness walk, breakfast, and return transport.

trips.title: Jinja Adrenaline Adventure
trips.summary: Return transport, one-night accommodation, white-water rafting, a Source of the Nile visit, photography, and breakfast. Accommodation upgrades are available.

trips.title: Sipi Falls & Mount Elgon Escape
trips.summary: Accommodation, transport, a guided waterfall hike, coffee experience, nature walks, meals, and photography in Eastern Uganda.

trips.title: Fort Portal Nature & Wellness Experience
trips.summary: Luxury accommodation, return transport, crater lake tour, tea plantation walk, waterfall visit, nature walks, breakfast, photography, and a local host.

posts.title: What to Pack for a Uganda Safari
posts.author: Explore Bloom & Beyond Team
posts.excerpt: A practical checklist for game drives, gorilla trekking, lodge stays, and changing weather.
posts.content: Pack comfortable layers, neutral clothing, sunscreen, insect repellent, binoculars, reusable water bottle, camera batteries, and a small day bag. For gorilla trekking, add hiking shoes, garden gloves, rain gear, and long sleeves.

posts.title: A First-Timer Guide to Zanzibar
posts.author: Explore Bloom & Beyond Team
posts.excerpt: How to mix beach relaxation with Stone Town, spice tours, and local food experiences.
posts.content: Spend time on the beach, visit Stone Town, tour spice farms, try local seafood, and plan boat activities around tide conditions. Zanzibar pairs beautifully with a safari in Tanzania, Kenya, or Uganda.

posts.title: How to Plan a Smooth Group Trip
posts.author: Explore Bloom & Beyond Team
posts.excerpt: Simple planning tips for family holidays, company retreats, and school trips.
posts.content: Choose a clear budget, collect traveler details early, agree on must-have activities, and assign one group contact person. A shared itinerary and early rooming list make the experience smoother.

posts.title: Best Time to Visit Uganda for Safaris and Gorillas
posts.author: Explore Bloom & Beyond Team
posts.excerpt: A seasonal guide for wildlife viewing, gorilla trekking, family travel, and photography.
posts.content: Uganda is a year-round destination, but many travelers prefer the drier months for easier trekking and game drives. Green seasons can offer lush scenery, birdlife, and fewer crowds.

posts.title: Safari Honeymoon Ideas for East Africa
posts.author: Explore Bloom & Beyond Team
posts.excerpt: Romantic ways to combine wildlife, private lodges, beaches, sunset cruises, and slow travel.
posts.content: A strong honeymoon itinerary balances adventure and rest. Pair a private safari lodge with beach days, scenic flights, candle-lit dinners, and flexible transfers so the trip never feels rushed.

posts.title: How to Budget for an African Safari
posts.author: Explore Bloom & Beyond Team
posts.excerpt: Understand permits, accommodation, transport, park fees, meals, and private guide costs before booking.
posts.content: Your safari budget depends on season, accommodation style, transport distance, permits, park fees, group size, and guide level. Sharing your budget early helps the travel team design realistic options.
